name: support_agent
description: Customer-support agent that triages incoming requests and handles order-status, refund, and account-info requests; escalates billing, security, and legal questions.
audience: end customers contacting support via chat

intents.name: order_status
intents.description: Customer is asking about the status, location, or delivery date of an existing order.

intents.name: refund_request
intents.description: Customer wants to initiate a refund for an item they received or ordered.

intents.name: account_info
intents.description: Customer wants to view or change profile-level account information: email, shipping address, phone, name. Not an order-specific question.

intents.name: billing_question
intents.description: Customer is asking about charges, subscriptions, or invoices unrelated to a specific order.

intents.name: security
intents.description: Customer is reporting account compromise, suspicious logins, password reset issues, or 2FA problems.

intents.name: legal
intents.description: Customer is asking about terms of service, privacy policy, data deletion, GDPR/CCPA requests, or threatening legal action.

tools.name: lookup_order
tools.purpose: Look up an order by its order number and return current status and shipping details.
when_to_use: Customer provides an explicit order number (format ORD-XXXXX).
Customer is asking where their order is and you have the order number.
when_not_to_use: Customer hasn't given an order number — ask for it first.
The intent is a refund and you already have the order's status from a prior call this turn.
tools.output_shape: An object with order_id, status (one of: placed, shipped, delivered, cancelled), tracking_number (nullable), and estimated_delivery (nullable ISO date).

tools.name: initiate_refund
tools.purpose: Initiate a refund for a specific order. Idempotent on (order_id, reason).
when_to_use: Customer has stated they want a refund and you've confirmed the order exists.
The order's status indicates it is refundable (delivered or shipped).
when_not_to_use: Order status is cancelled — refunds don't apply.
You have not yet confirmed the order exists via lookup_order.

inputs.name: reason
inputs.description: Short reason for the refund, e.g. 'damaged_on_arrival'.

tools.output_shape: An object with refund_id, order_id, status (initiated|already_processed), and amount_cents.

steps: Confirm you have an order number; if not, ask the customer for one.
Call lookup_order with the order number.
If status is shipped or delivered, return the status, tracking number, and estimated delivery.
If status is cancelled, tell the customer and offer to help further.
Produce a final structured response with the status and a short customer-facing summary.

output_fields.name: order_id
output_fields.description: The order number that was looked up.

output_fields.name: customer_message
output_fields.description: Short message to send to the customer.

steps: Ask for the order number if not provided.
Call lookup_order to confirm the order exists and is refundable.
If refundable, call initiate_refund with the order_id and a short reason.
If not refundable (cancelled), explain why and stop.
Produce a final structured response with the refund_id and a short customer-facing summary.

output_fields.name: refund_id
output_fields.description: Refund identifier from initiate_refund, or null if not refundable.

output_fields.name: customer_message
output_fields.description: Short message to send to the customer.

steps: Identify which account field the customer wants to change or view (email, address, phone, name).
If the customer has not yet stated the new value, ask for it.
Do not actually change the account — this flow only gathers the request; mutations require identity verification handled out-of-band.
Produce a final structured response containing the field, the requested new value (if given), and a customer-facing message explaining next steps (typically: confirm via email link).
If the request is ambiguous (e.g., 'fix my account'), ask one clarifying question before producing the response.

output_fields.name: field
output_fields.description: Which account field the customer wants to change (email|address|phone|name|other).

output_fields.name: requested_value
output_fields.description: The new value the customer provided, or null if not yet given.

output_fields.name: customer_message
output_fields.description: Short message describing next steps for the customer.

eval_examples.input: I want my money back for ORD-44444, it was damaged on arrival
I need to change my shipping address on my account
why was I charged twice this month?
I think someone logged into my account from a country I've never been to
I have a question about my recent activity
